title: Mockup Command
description: Add sl mockup [spec-name] and sl mockup update commands with a streamlined interactive TUI flow. Auto-detects spec from branch, auto-detects framework (no confirmation needed), checks/generates design system (CSS tokens only - no component indexing), builds an AI agent prompt, launches the agent to generate mockups (HTML or JSX), and offers post-agent commit/push. The AI agent discovers components via codebase search. Shared editor/prompt utilities extracted from revise into pkg/cli/prompt/. Extends sl init for frontend projects.

title: Phase 1: Shared Infrastructure - Extract prompt/editor from revise
description: Extract shared editor and prompt utilities from pkg/cli/revise/ into a new reusable pkg/cli/prompt/ package. Refactor revise to delegate to the shared package. This enables both revise and mockup to share editor integration, template rendering, and token estimation. Implements FR-020.

title: Phase 2: Setup - Package and Command Skeleton
description: Initialize the pkg/cli/mockup/ domain package with shared types (including MockupPromptContext, PromptComponent, SpecContent) and create the Cobra command skeleton with all flags (--format, --force, --dry-run, --summary, --json). Register VarMockupCmd in cmd/sl/main.go. Spec-name argument is optional.

title: Phase 3: Domain Logic - Detector, Style Scanner, Design System, Spec Parser
description: Core domain logic: frontend framework detector (tiered heuristics), CSS token extractor (colors, fonts, variables - no component scanning), design system file I/O (YAML frontmatter + markdown), and spec parser (extract SpecContent from spec.md). Component discovery delegated to AI agent.

title: Phase 4: Prompt and Template - Prompt builder, template, golden tests
description: Create the AI agent prompt template (prompt.tmpl), implement MockupPromptContext builder that assembles context from gathered data and renders the template, and add golden file tests to verify prompt output for various contexts (React HTML, React JSX, Vue HTML, empty components).

title: Phase 5: Interactive Flow - Streamlined 6-step wiring in mockup.go
description: Wire the streamlined interactive flow: spec resolution (arg/branch/picker), auto framework detection (no confirmation), design system check/generate (CSS tokens), prompt generation, editor review with action menu, agent launch with fallback, post-agent commit/push. Format uses --format flag (no interactive selection).

title: Phase 6: Update Command - sl mockup update handler
description: sl mockup update re-extracts CSS tokens with interactive confirmation and writes the updated design_system.md. No component scanning - focuses on design tokens only.

title: Phase 7: Init Integration - Initialize Design System During Onboarding
description: Extend sl init/bootstrap flow to detect frontend projects and auto-create specledger/design_system.md during onboarding. Interactive prompt in TUI mode, auto-create in CI mode.

title: Phase 8: Polish - JSON Output, Edge Cases, Tests
description: Cross-cutting concerns: --json non-interactive path for both commands, comprehensive error messages (agent not found, mockup exists, user cancel, malformed design system), edge case handling, and unit tests for all domain logic including prompt package.

title: Phase 3: US1 - Generate Mockup from Spec Using Design System [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED by Phase 4 (SL-d4e5f6) and Phase 5 (SL-f7a8b9). The non-interactive generator approach was replaced by an interactive TUI flow with AI agent-driven mockup generation.

title: Phase 4: US2 - Auto-Create Design System Index When Missing [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. Auto-create logic is now part of the interactive flow in Phase 5 (SL-f7a8b9, task T012). The design system generation is handled as an interactive step within the main mockup flow.

title: Phase 5: US3 - Frontend Detection and Design System Init [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. Frontend detection with interactive confirmation is now task T011 in Phase 5 (SL-f7a8b9). The --force bypass and ambiguous repo handling are included in that task.

title: Implement mockup generator with ASCII screens [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. The AI agent now generates mockup content, not Go code. This task was replaced by T007 (prompt template) and T008 (prompt builder) in Phase 4.

title: Wire sl mockup command handler with sequential flow [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. The non-interactive sequential handler was replaced by the 10-step interactive flow in Phase 5 (tasks T010-T016).

title: Add auto-generation flow when design_system.md is missing [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. Auto-generation is now part of the interactive flow in T012 (design system check/generate with interactive prompts).

title: Add third-party library detection [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. Third-party library detection is now incorporated directly into T004 (component scanner) as part of the scanner's core functionality.

title: Integrate frontend detection into mockup command [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. Detection integration with interactive confirmation is now T011 in Phase 5.

title: Handle ambiguous repos and monorepo detection [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. Ambiguous repo handling is now incorporated into T003 (detector) and T011 (interactive confirmation).

title: Add merge logic for design system updates [SUPERSEDED]
description: SUPERSEDED. Merge logic is now incorporated directly into T005 (design system I/O) as MergeDesignSystem function.

title: Mockup Command
description:
Add sl mockup [spec-name] and sl mockup update commands with interactive TUI flow. The command auto-detects the spec from the branch, auto-detects the frontend framework, checks/generates design system (CSS tokens), builds an AI agent prompt from templates and context, and lets the user review before launching the agent. The AI agent generates the mockup (HTML or JSX), and the user can optionally commit/push the result.

Key capabilities:
- Interactive TUI flow for mockup generation
- Automatic frontend framework detection (React/Vue/Svelte/Angular)
- Design system extraction (CSS tokens, variables, theme colors)
- AI agent prompt generation and review
- Post-agent commit/push flow
- Integration with sl init for onboarding

User Stories:
- US1 (P1): Generate Mockup via Interactive Flow
- US2 (P2): Auto-Create Design System When Missing
- US3 (P2): Automatic Framework Detection
- US4 (P2): Update Design System
- US5 (P3): Initialize Design System During Onboarding

title: Phase 1: Setup
description:
Project initialization and shared utility extraction.

WHY: Before implementing the mockup command, we need to set up the package structure and extract shared utilities from the revise package into a reusable pkg/cli/prompt/ package. This enables code reuse between revise and mockup commands.

DESIGN: 
- Create pkg/cli/mockup/ directory for domain logic
- Create pkg/cli/prompt/ directory for shared editor/prompt utilities
- Extract editor detection and prompt editing from pkg/cli/revise/

ACCEPTANCE:
- pkg/cli/mockup/ package exists with types.go
- pkg/cli/prompt/ package exists with editor.go and prompt.go
- Existing revise command still works after refactoring

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] pkg/cli/mockup/ directory created
[ ] pkg/cli/prompt/ directory created
[ ] Shared utilities extracted from revise
[ ] Revise command regression tested

title: Create pkg/cli/mockup package structure
description:
WHY: The mockup command needs a dedicated package for domain logic following the established pattern of pkg/cli/revise/, pkg/cli/session/.

DESIGN: Create pkg/cli/mockup/ directory with types.go file containing all type definitions from data-model.md (FrameworkType, DetectionResult, MockupFormat, DesignSystem, StyleInfo, SpecContent, MockupPromptContext, MockupResult, UpdateResult).

ACCEPTANCE: 
- pkg/cli/mockup/types.go exists with all type definitions
- Types match data-model.md specifications
- Package compiles without errors

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] pkg/cli/mockup/ directory created
[ ] types.go file created with all type definitions
[ ] go build succeeds

title: Extract shared editor utilities to pkg/cli/prompt
description:
WHY: Both revise and mockup commands need editor detection and prompt editing functionality. Extracting to a shared package avoids code duplication.

DESIGN: 
- Create pkg/cli/prompt/editor.go with DetectEditor() and EditPrompt() functions
- Create pkg/cli/prompt/editor_test.go with unit tests
- Move logic from pkg/cli/revise/editor.go to the new package
- Update pkg/cli/revise/editor.go to delegate to pkg/cli/prompt/editor.go

ACCEPTANCE:
- pkg/cli/prompt/editor.go exports DetectEditor() and EditPrompt()
- Existing revise command works without changes to calling code
- Unit tests pass

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] pkg/cli/prompt/editor.go created
[ ] pkg/cli/prompt/editor_test.go created
[ ] pkg/cli/revise/editor.go refactored to use shared package
[ ] revise command regression tested

title: Extract shared prompt utilities to pkg/cli/prompt
description:
WHY: Template rendering and token estimation are needed by both revise and mockup commands. Extracting to a shared package enables reuse.

DESIGN:
- Create pkg/cli/prompt/prompt.go with RenderTemplate(), EstimateTokens(), PrintTokenWarnings()
- Create pkg/cli/prompt/prompt_test.go with unit tests
- Move relevant logic from pkg/cli/revise/prompt.go to the new package
- Update pkg/cli/revise/prompt.go to delegate to pkg/cli/prompt/prompt.go

ACCEPTANCE:
- pkg/cli/prompt/prompt.go exports template rendering functions
- Token estimation works correctly
- Existing revise command works without changes to calling code

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] pkg/cli/prompt/prompt.go created
[ ] pkg/cli/prompt/prompt_test.go created
[ ] pkg/cli/revise/prompt.go refactored to use shared package
[ ] Unit tests pass

title: Phase 2: Foundational - Core Infrastructure
description:
Core infrastructure that MUST be complete before ANY user story can be implemented.

WHY: The mockup command depends on framework detection, CSS token extraction, and design system I/O. These are shared capabilities needed by all user stories.

DESIGN:
- pkg/cli/mockup/detector.go - Framework detection with tiered heuristics
- pkg/cli/mockup/stylescan.go - CSS token extraction (colors, fonts, variables)
- pkg/cli/mockup/designsystem.go - Design system read/write (YAML frontmatter + markdown)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Framework detection works for React/Next.js/Vue/Nuxt/Svelte/SvelteKit/Angular
- CSS tokens extracted from Tailwind config, CSS variables, theme files
- Design system can be read and written in correct format

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] detector.go implemented and tested
[ ] stylescan.go implemented and tested
[ ] designsystem.go implemented and tested
[ ] All unit tests pass

title: Implement framework detector
description:
WHY: The mockup command needs to detect the frontend framework to generate appropriate mockups and validate the project is a frontend project.

DESIGN:
- Create pkg/cli/mockup/detector.go with DetectFramework(projectPath string) function
- Implement tiered detection:
  - Tier 1: Config files (next.config.js, angular.json, svelte.config.js, etc.) - 97-99% confidence
  - Tier 2: package.json dependencies (react, vue, @angular/core, svelte) - 85% confidence
  - Tier 3: File extension scan (.tsx, .jsx, .vue, .svelte) - 70% confidence
- Return DetectionResult with IsFrontend, Framework, Confidence, ComponentDirs, Indicators
- Skip node_modules/, vendor/, .git/, dist/, build/ directories

ACCEPTANCE:
- Detects React/Next.js/Vue/Nuxt/Svelte/SvelteKit/Angular correctly
- Returns confidence score 0-100
- IsFrontend=true only when confidence >= 70
- Performance: < 2 seconds for typical codebase

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] detector.go created with DetectFramework function
[ ] Tier 1 config file detection implemented
[ ] Tier 2 package.json detection implemented
[ ] Tier 3 file extension scan implemented
[ ] detector_test.go with table-driven tests

title: Implement CSS token scanner
description:
WHY: The design system needs to capture the project visual identity through CSS tokens (colors, fonts, spacing, variables) so mockups match the project style.

DESIGN:
- Create pkg/cli/mockup/stylescan.go with ScanStyles(projectPath string) function
- Extract tokens from:
  - Tailwind CSS: tailwind.config.js/ts (theme.colors, theme.fontFamily, theme.extend)
  - CSS Variables: src/index.css, src/globals.css, src/styles/*.css (--color-*, --font-*, --spacing-*)
  - CSS-in-JS: src/theme.ts, src/styles/theme.js (styled-components, emotion, Chakra themes)
- Return StyleInfo with CSSFramework, Preprocessor, StylingApproach, ThemeColors, FontFamilies, CSSVariables

ACCEPTANCE:
- Extracts Tailwind CSS theme colors and fonts
- Extracts CSS custom properties from global stylesheets
- Identifies styling approach (utility-first, css-in-js, css-modules)
- Performance: < 5 seconds for typical codebase

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] stylescan.go created with ScanStyles function
[ ] Tailwind config parsing implemented
[ ] CSS variables extraction implemented
[ ] CSS-in-JS theme detection implemented
[ ] stylescan_test.go with unit tests

title: Implement design system read/write
description:
WHY: The design system file stores CSS tokens in a structured format that both humans and the system can read/update. Manual edits must be preserved.

DESIGN:
- Create pkg/cli/mockup/designsystem.go with:
  - ReadDesignSystem(path string) (*DesignSystem, error)
  - WriteDesignSystem(path string, ds *DesignSystem) error
  - InitializeDesignSystem(projectPath string, detection *DetectionResult) error
- File format: YAML frontmatter + markdown body
- Location: .specledger/memory/design-system.md
- Preserve manual sections marked with <!-- MANUAL --> markers

ACCEPTANCE:
- Reads design system with YAML frontmatter
- Writes design system preserving manual sections
- Creates new design system from detection result
- Handles malformed files gracefully (warn and regenerate)

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] designsystem.go created with read/write functions
[ ] YAML frontmatter parsing implemented
[ ] Manual section preservation implemented
[ ] Malformed file handling implemented
[ ] designsystem_test.go with unit tests

title: Implement spec parser
description:
WHY: The mockup prompt needs parsed content from spec.md including title, user stories, and requirements to generate contextual mockups.

DESIGN:
- Create pkg/cli/mockup/specparser.go with ParseSpec(specPath string) (*SpecContent, error)
- Extract:
  - Title from first H1 heading
  - UserStories from ### User Story sections
  - Requirements from ### Functional Requirements section
  - FullContent as raw markdown (fallback if parsing fails)
- Validate spec has user scenarios (required for mockup)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Parses title from H1 heading
- Extracts user story sections with priorities
- Extracts functional requirements
- Returns FullContent as fallback
- Returns error if no user scenarios found

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] specparser.go created with ParseSpec function
[ ] Title extraction implemented
[ ] User story extraction implemented
[ ] Requirements extraction implemented
[ ] specparser_test.go with unit tests

title: Phase 3: US1 - Generate Mockup via Interactive Flow (MVP)
description:
User Story 1 (P1): As a frontend developer working on a feature, I want to run sl mockup [spec-name] so that an interactive flow guides me through design system setup and prompt review before launching an AI agent to generate a mockup grounded in my project design tokens.

WHY: This is the core value proposition - an agent-driven flow that generates mockups grounded in the project design tokens ensures consistency and produces higher-quality output.

DESIGN:
- pkg/cli/commands/mockup.go - Cobra command definitions
- pkg/cli/mockup/mockupprompt.go - Prompt context builder and template renderer
- pkg/cli/mockup/prompt.tmpl - Go template for AI agent instructions
- cmd/sl/main.go - Command registration

ACCEPTANCE:
- sl mockup auto-detects spec from branch or accepts explicit spec name
- Interactive flow shows framework detection, design system status
- Editor opens for prompt review with action menu
- Agent launches with prompt via launcher.LaunchWithPrompt()
- Post-agent commit/push flow using stagingAndCommitFlow pattern

DEFINITION OF DONE:
[ ] mockup.go Cobra command created
[ ] mockupprompt.go prompt builder implemented
[ ] prompt.tmpl template created
[ ] Command registered in main.go
[ ] Interactive flow works end-to-end
[ ] Agent launch works with fallback to file
